info.name: ISP Management Platform API
info.description:
Full platform collection: internal AAA (FreeRADIUS-facing) endpoints plus the full admin-facing API (Accounts/Customers/Plans/Subscribers/Sessions/Accounting/Billing/Payments/Network/Dashboard) — this last group is a Django port of isp-express-main's /api/v1/* admin modules.

**Auth models used here:**
- `aaa/*` (Authenticate/Authorize/Accounting/Post-Auth/Disconnect/CoA) endpoints use the `x-internal-api-key` header (see `INTERNAL_API_KEY` in config/settings.py).
- Everything else uses JWT bearer auth. Run **Accounts > Login** first — its test script stores the token into the `adminToken` collection variable automatically, and every other admin request sends `Authorization: Bearer {{adminToken}}`.

**One-time setup:**
```
manage.py ensure_default_admin
```
This seeds the admin user from `DEFAULT_ADMIN_EMAIL` / `DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD` (defaults: admin@example.com / Admin@123, matching the `adminEmail` / `adminPassword` variables below).

**Self-seeding flow** for a fresh database: run **Accounts > Login**, then **Customers > Create Customer**, **Plans > Create Plan**, **Subscribers > Create Subscriber** in order — each stores its new id (`customerId`, `planId`, `subscriberId`) into collection variables automatically, so Billing/Sessions/Accounting/Network/Dashboard requests further down all pick them up with no manual steps. The AAA folder (Authenticate/Authorize) still expects specific `pppoeUsername`/`hotspotUsername`/`macAddress` subscribers to exist — either create matching ones via the Subscribers folder, or seed via shell:
```python
from common.passwords import hash_password
from customers.models import Customer
from plans.models import Plan
from subscribers.models import Subscriber

c = Customer.objects.create(customer_code='C-TEST', full_name='Test Customer', phone='1234567890', address='Addr', city='City')
p = Plan.objects.create(plan_code='P-TEST', name='Test Plan', monthly_fee=10, speed_profile_name='10M', download_rate_kbps=10240, upload_rate_kbps=10240)

Subscriber.objects.create(subscriber_code='S-PPPOE', customer=c, plan=p, username='jdoe', password_hash=hash_password('secret'), service_type='pppoe', installation_address='Addr', mac_address='AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF')
Subscriber.objects.create(subscriber_code='S-HOTSPOT', customer=c, plan=p, username='hsuser', password_hash=hash_password('secret'), service_type='hotspot', installation_address='Addr')
```

exec: pm.test('service type mismatch reason', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().replyMessage).to.eql('Subscriber is not provisioned for this service type'));

item.name: Validation Error - No Username Or MAC

exec: const match = body.find((a) => String(a.subscriber) === String(subscriberId)) || body[0];
